title: AuditFinding
description: A single structured finding emitted by a Research-OS audit. One JSON object per atomic check result. Audits write a list of these per run; the writer fans the list out to a human-readable .md, a schema-validated .json, and a one-line-per-finding append-only .jsonl.

id.description: Unique identifier for this finding (UUIDv4 hex string).

audit_name.description: Stable identifier for the audit that produced this finding (e.g. 'step_completeness', 'claim_grounding', 'prose_quality').

severity.description: block = halts the gate; warn = surfaces but does not halt; info = informational only.

dimension.description: The substantive axis being checked (e.g. 'completeness', 'grounding', 'reproducibility', 'prose', 'figures'). Used for grouping in reports.

evidence_paths.description: Workspace-relative paths to the artefacts the finding refers to (e.g. 'workspace/01_eda/conclusions.md').

suggested_fix.description: Actionable one-to-three sentence remediation the AI or user can apply.

override_kwarg.description: Name of the kwarg on the originating tool that, when set true with a rationale, bypasses this finding. null if the finding has no override path.

override_log_format.description: Template (Python str.format-style) for the line written to the override audit trail when the override is used. null if no override exists.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the finding was produced.

ro_version.description: research_os.__version__ at the time the finding was produced; used to attribute findings to a specific release.
